figures.caption: FIG. 1. Phase diagram of MoTe2/WSe2 at hole filling ν = νf + νc = 2 and T = 0 with varying displacement field ∆. The top abscissa shows the displacement field δ∆ = ∆(νf )−∆(νf = 1) relative to νf = 1, while all other abscissas show the equivalent f -band filling νf . The relation between δ∆ and νf is shown in the inset. In (a), we show the gap Eg and the anomalous spin-Hall conductance σxy s , while (b) shows the local f -band spin and charge susceptibilities χs and χc, respectively. The latter are an indication of the degree of correlations, since χc = 4χs in the non-interacting limit and χc ≪ 4χs in the Kondo limit. Based on those quantities, we distinguish four different regimes. A topological Kondo insulator (TKI, orange) with quantized σxy s = e2/h and strong correlations (χc ≪ 4χs) around νf = 1, a more weakly correlated topological insulator (TI, green) with σxy s = e2/h further away from νf = 1, a trivial band insulator (BI, gray) with σxy s = 0 far away from νf = 1, and a compensated topological semimetal (CTSM, blue) with Eg = 0 and non-zero but non-quantized σxy s , separating TI from BI in the νf < 1 region. TI and TKI correspond to the same phase and are only qualitatively distinguished by the degree of their correlations. We use 4χs/χc > 20 for the TKI and 4χs/χc < 10 for the TI, with a crossover region in between.

FIG. 3. (a) the gap Eg, the SLCs Js, and the f band scattering rate τ−1 f = −ImΣf loc(ω = 0) versus temperature. (b) Resistivity (blue) and spin Hall conductance (orange) versus temperature. The red dashed line: Arrhenius fit for T ≤ 2K. (c) Total spectral function Ak↑(ω) = Afk↑(ω) + Ack↑(ω) at T = 15 K; the inset shows Ak↑(0). (d) real part of detG −1 k↑ (0) and (e) valence-band Berry curvature of Heff k↑ [c.f. Eq. (12)].

FIG. 2. (a) spin loop currents (blue) and gap (orange) versus Uf nn. (b) spin Hall conductance versus Uf nn. (c,d) Total spectral function Ak↑(ω) = Afk↑(ω) + Ack↑(ω), in (c) the CTSM and (d) the TKI phase. The insets show Ak↑(0) (Fermi surface). All results are obtained at T = 0.
